claim: H.pylori-mediated gastric cancer occurs due to the accumulation of mutations in host epithelial cells.
evidence_sentences: H. pylori–mediated upregulation of AID resulted in the accumulation of nucleotide alterations in the TP53 tumor suppressor gene in gastric cells in vitro.

Our findings provide evidence that aberrant AID expression caused by H. pylori infection might be a mechanism of mutation accumulation in the gastric mucosa during H. pylori–associated gastric carcinogenesis.
